description: Quantum optics quantizes the electromagnetic field into photon number states and characterizes light by photon statistics: a Hanbury Brown-Twiss setup measures the second-order correlation g2(0), distinguishing coherent, thermal, and single-photon sources, where antibunching with g2(0) < 1 signals genuinely nonclassical light.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Quantize EM field"]
    N2["Photon number states |n>"]
    N3{"Choose light source"}
    N4["Coherent state (laser)"]
    N5["Single-photon source"]
    N6["Thermal source"]
    N7["Hanbury Brown-Twiss"]
    N8["Measure g2(0)"]
    N9{"g2(0) < 1?"}
    N10["Antibunched: nonclassical"]
    N11["Photon statistics result"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|coherent| N4
    N3 -->|single| N5
    N3 -->|thermal| N6
    N4 --> N7
    N5 --> N7
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 -->|yes| N10
    N10 --> N11
    N9 -->|no| N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
